content: We can frame Self-Refine as the iterative, step-by-step improvement mechanic of LLM outputs, and Reflexion's dynamic memory as the accumulated long-term context that anchors each round of self-feedback, creating a two-layer self-improvement system for autonomous agents that outperforms either approach alone. This system lets agents carry over lessons learned from past refinement iterations to avoid repeating mistakes in new self-feedback rounds.
